```text
     1→package geminicli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"crypto/sha256"
     5→	"encoding/hex"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"os"
    10→	"path/filepath"
    11→	"sort"
    12→	"strings"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    15→)
    16→
    17→var (
    18→	osGetwd = os.Getwd
    19→)
    20→
    21→// GeminiPathError describes actionable filesystem failures when locating Gemini data.
    22→type GeminiPathError struct {
    23→	Kind        string   // tmp_missing, project_missing
    24→	Path        string   // offending path
    25→	ProjectHash string   // computed hash when relevant
    26→	KnownHashes []string // hashes discovered on disk (optional)
    27→	Message     string   // user-facing explanation
    28→}
    29→
    30→func (e *GeminiPathError) Error() string {
    31→	if e == nil {
    32→		return "<nil>"
    33→	}
    34→	return e.Message
    35→}
    36→
    37→// HashProjectPath deterministically hashes an absolute project path using SHA256.
    38→// On case-insensitive filesystems (macOS), it resolves the path to its canonical
    39→// form with the correct case to ensure consistent hashing.
    40→func HashProjectPath(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    41→	if projectPath == "" {
    42→		return "", fmt.Errorf("project path is empty")
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	absPath, err := filepath.Abs(projectPath)
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve absolute path for %q: %w", projectPath, err)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	// Resolve to canonical path with correct case (important for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
    51→	canonicalPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(absPath)
    52→	if err != nil {
    53→		// If getting canonical path fails, use absPath as fallback
    54→		slog.Warn("HashProjectPath: Failed to get canonical path, using absolute path",
    55→			"absPath", absPath,
    56→			"error", err)
    57→		canonicalPath = absPath
    58→	} else if canonicalPath != absPath {
    59→		// Log when the path was canonicalized (case was corrected)
    60→		slog.Debug("HashProjectPath: Resolved path to canonical form",
    61→			"original", absPath,
    62→			"canonical", canonicalPath)
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(canonicalPath))
    66→	return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil
    67→}
    68→
    69→// GetGeminiTmpDir returns the path to the Gemini tmp directory
    70→func GetGeminiTmpDir() (string, error) {
    71→	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
    72→	if err != nil {
    73→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
    74→	}
    75→	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".gemini", "tmp"), nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→// GetGeminiProjectDir returns the Gemini project directory for the given path.
    79→// It calculates the SHA256 hash of the project path.
    80→func GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    81→	if projectPath == "" {
    82→		var err error
    83→		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
    84→		if err != nil {
    85→			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
    86→		}
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	projectHash, err := HashProjectPath(projectPath)
    90→	if err != nil {
    91→		return "", err
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
    95→	if err != nil {
    96→		return "", err
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	resolvedDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, projectHash)
   100→
   101→	slog.Debug("GetGeminiProjectDir: Computed Gemini project directory",
   102→		"projectPath", projectPath,
   103→		"projectHash", projectHash,
   104→		"tmpDir", tmpDir,
   105→		"resolvedDir", resolvedDir)
   106→
   107→	return resolvedDir, nil
   108→}
   109→
   110→// ResolveGeminiProjectDir ensures both tmp and hash directories exist on disk.
   111→func ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
   112→	projectDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
   113→	if err != nil {
   114→		return "", err
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	tmpDir := filepath.Dir(projectDir)
   118→
   119→	slog.Debug("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Checking for Gemini directories",
   120→		"tmpDir", tmpDir,
   121→		"projectDir", projectDir)
   122→
   123→	if _, err := osStat(tmpDir); err != nil {
   124→		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   125→			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini tmp directory not found", "tmpDir", tmpDir)
   126→			return "", &GeminiPathError{
   127→				Kind:    "tmp_missing",
   128→				Path:    tmpDir,
   129→				Message: fmt.Sprintf("Gemini tmp directory %q not found. Run the Gemini CLI at least once or verify ~/.gemini exists.", tmpDir),
   130→			}
   131→		}
   132→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	if _, err := osStat(projectDir); err != nil {
   136→		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   137→			hashes, listErr := ListGeminiProjectHashes()
   138→			if listErr != nil {
   139→				hashes = nil
   140→			}
   141→
   142→			var known string
   143→			if len(hashes) > 0 {
   144→				known = strings.Join(hashes, ", ")
   145→			} else {
   146→				known = "(none discovered)"
   147→			}
   148→
   149→			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini project directory not found",
   150→				"projectDir", projectDir,
   151→				"knownHashes", known)
   152→
   153→			return "", &GeminiPathError{
   154→				Kind:        "project_missing",
   155→				Path:        projectDir,
   156→				ProjectHash: filepath.Base(projectDir),
   157→				KnownHashes: hashes,
   158→				Message: fmt.Sprintf("No Gemini data found for this project (expected %q). Known project hashes: %s. Start a Gemini CLI session in your repo to create it.",
   159→					projectDir, known),
   160→			}
   161→		}
   162→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini project directory %q: %w", projectDir, err)
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Successfully resolved Gemini project directory", "projectDir", projectDir)
   166→
   167→	return projectDir, nil
   168→}
   169→
   170→// ListGeminiProjectHashes returns the list of project hash directories currently on disk.
   171→func ListGeminiProjectHashes() ([]string, error) {
   172→	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return nil, err
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
   178→	if err != nil {
   179→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	var hashes []string
   183→	for _, entry := range entries {
   184→		if entry.IsDir() {
   185→			hashes = append(hashes, entry.Name())
   186→		}
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	sort.Strings(hashes)
   190→	return hashes, nil
   191→}
   192→
```

```text
    10→
    11→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    12→)
    13→
    14→// Package-level variables for mocking in tests
    15→var (
    16→	osUserHomeDir = os.UserHomeDir
    17→	osStat        = os.Stat
    18→	execLookPath  = exec.LookPath
    19→)
    20→
    21→// getDefaultGeminiCommand returns the default gemini command.
    22→// It checks for gemini in PATH.
    23→func getDefaultGeminiCommand() string {
    24→	if _, err := execLookPath("gemini"); err == nil {
    25→		slog.Info("Found Gemini CLI in PATH")
    26→		return "gemini"
    27→	}
    28→	slog.Info("Gemini CLI not found in PATH, defaulting to 'gemini'")
    29→	return "gemini"
    30→}
    31→
    32→// parseGeminiCommand parses a custom command string into executable and arguments.
    33→func parseGeminiCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
    34→	if customCommand != "" {
    35→		parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
    36→		if len(parts) > 0 {
    37→			return parts[0], parts[1:]
    38→		}
    39→	}
    40→	return getDefaultGeminiCommand(), nil
    41→}
    42→
    43→func ensureResumeArgs(args []string, resumeSessionID string) []string {
    44→	if resumeSessionID == "" {
    45→		return args
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	for i, arg := range args {
    49→		if arg == "--resume" || arg == "-r" {
    50→			if i+1 < len(args) {
    51→				return args
    52→			}
    53→		}
    54→		if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--resume=") {
    55→			return args
    56→		}
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	return append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionID)
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ExecuteGemini runs the Gemini CLI with the given arguments
    63→func ExecuteGemini(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
    64→	// Parse the command and any custom arguments
    65→	geminiCmd, customArgs := parseGeminiCommand(customCommand)
    66→
    67→	customArgs = ensureResumeArgs(customArgs, resumeSessionID)
    68→	if resumeSessionID != "" {
    69→		slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Passing resume argument", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	// Create the command
    73→	cmd := exec.Command(geminiCmd, customArgs...)
    74→
    75→	// Set up stdin/stdout/stderr to match the parent process
    76→	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    77→	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    78→	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    79→
    80→	// Start the command
    81→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Starting Gemini CLI process")
    82→	if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
    83→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start gemini: %v", err)
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	// Wait for the command to complete
    87→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Waiting for Gemini CLI to exit")
    88→	if err := cmd.Wait(); err != nil {
    89→		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
    90→			exitCode := exitErr.ExitCode()
    91→			slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Gemini CLI exited", "exitCode", exitCode)
    92→			os.Exit(exitCode)
    93→		}
    94→		return fmt.Errorf("gemini execution failed: %v", err)
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Gemini CLI exited normally", "exitCode", 0)
    98→	return nil
    99→}
   100→
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-02 22:48:24Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings.

## Gemini CLI Provider Implementation Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of the Gemini and Claude Code provider implementations. Here's what I found:

### 1. Directory Watching/Polling Mechanisms

**Gemini Provider (`/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/watcher.go`):**

The Gemini provider uses an **active polling mechanism with exponential backoff** before switching to file system watching:

- **Initial Phase (Polling with Backoff):**
  - Implements a `waitForDirectory()` function (lines 241-267) that polls for directory existence
  - Starts with 1 second backoff and exponentially increases to a maximum of 30 seconds
  - Monitors three directories sequentially:
    1. Gemini tmp directory (`~/.gemini/tmp`)
    2. Project directory (hash-based subdirectory)
    3. Chats directory within the project

- **Constant Values:**
  - `initialBackoff = 1 * time.Second`
  - `maxBackoff = 30 * time.Second`

- **Once Directories Exist:**
  - Switches to `fsnotify` for real-time file system watching
  - Watches for `.json` file creates/writes in the chats directory
  - Also sets up artifact watchers for `logs.json` and `shell_history`

**Claude Code Provider (`/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/claudecode/watcher.go`):**

Claude Code uses **immediate file system watching** with reactive polling:

- **No Explicit Backoff:**
  - No polling loop like Gemini
  - Directly attempts to watch the project directory
  - If the directory doesn't exist, watches the parent directory

- **Fallback Behavior:**
  - If Claude projects directory is missing entirely, calls `WatchForClaudeSetup()`
  - This watches `~/.claude` or home directory for the initial setup
  - Once setup directories are created, transitions to normal project watcher

- **Watch Strategy:**
  - Uses `fsnotify` directly on the project directory
  - Listens for `.jsonl` file creates/writes
  - No active polling between checks

### 2. Logging Messages About Waiting for Directories

**Gemini Provider Log Messages:**

```
- "Gemini watcher: directory ready" (line 246) - logs when directory is found
- "Gemini watcher: waiting for directory" (line 252) - logs polling attempts with retry interval
- "Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for tmp directory" (line 88) - when context cancels
- "Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for project directory" (line 93)
- "Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for chats directory" (line 98)
```

**Claude Code Provider Log Messages:**

```
- "Waiting for JSONL file system events" (line 129) - ready to watch, no polling
- "Context cancelled while waiting for directory creation" (line 152)
- "Initial scan disabled; waiting for JSONL events" (line 171)
- "Claude directory not found, watching for creation of ~/.claude" (line 380)
- "Claude directory found, watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects" (line 386)
```

### 3. Key Differences

| Aspect | Gemini Provider | Claude Code Provider |
|--------|-----------------|---------------------|
| **Initial Strategy** | Polling with exponential backoff | Direct file system watching |
| **Backoff Implementation** | Active loop with `time.After()` | No polling; reactive only |
| **Backoff Range** | 1s → 30s (exponential) | N/A |
| **Directory Prerequisites** | Checks 3 directories sequentially | Checks project directory, watches parent if needed |
| **File Event Type** | Watches `.json` files | Watches `.jsonl` files |
| **Artifact Monitoring** | Additional watchers for logs.json, shell_history | No artifact watchers |
| **Setup Fallback** | Implicit (built into waitForDirectory) | Explicit `WatchForClaudeSetup()` function |

### Why These Differences Exist

- **Gemini:** Uses polling because Gemini creates hash-based project directories under `~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/` - these are slower to create and may not exist when the watcher starts
- **Claude Code:** Project directory is more reliably present or quickly created, so immediate watching works better; has explicit setup handling for first-time usage

The Gemini approach is more defensive (waits actively), while Claude Code is more optimistic (assumes directory will appear quickly and reacts to it).

```text
    71→func WatchGeminiProject(projectPath string, callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {

    73→	SetWatcherWorkspaceRoot(projectPath)

    75→	projectDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)

    81→	chatsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, "chats")
    82→
    83→	watcherWg.Add(1)
    84→	go func() {
    85→		defer watcherWg.Done()
    86→
    87→		if err := waitForDirectory(watcherCtx, tmpDir, "Gemini tmp directory"); err != nil {
    88→			slog.Warn("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for tmp directory", "error", err)

    90→		}
    91→

    93→			slog.Warn("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for project directory", "error", err)
    94→			return
    95→		}
    96→

   100→		}
   101→

   104→		}
   105→

   107→			slog.Warn("Failed to watch logs.json", "error", err)
   108→		}
   109→
   110→		if err := startArtifactWatcher(filepath.Join(projectDir, "shell_history"), "shell_history"); err != nil {
   111→			slog.Warn("Failed to watch shell_history", "error", err)
   112→		}
   113→	}()
   114→
   115→	return nil
   116→}
   117→
   118→func startChatsWatcher(chatsDir string) error {
   119→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   120→	if err != nil {
   121→		return err
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   125→	go func() {
   126→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   127→		defer func() {
   128→			_ = watcher.Close()
   129→		}()
   130→
   131→		if err := watcher.Add(chatsDir); err != nil {
   132→			slog.Error("Failed to add chats dir to watcher", "error", err)
   133→			return
   134→		}
   135→
   136→		for {
   137→			select {
   138→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   139→				return
   140→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   141→				if !ok {
   142→					return
   143→				}
   144→				if strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".json") && (event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create)) {
   145→					processSessionChange(event.Name)
   146→				}
   147→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   148→				if !ok {
   149→					return
   150→				}
   151→				slog.Error("Watcher error", "error", err)
   152→			}
   153→		}
   154→	}()
   155→
   156→	return nil
   157→}
   158→
   159→func processSessionChange(filePath string) {
   160→	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Detected session file change", "file", filePath)
   161→
   162→	// Extract short session ID from filename to find related files
   163→	filename := filepath.Base(filePath)
   164→	shortID := extractSessionIDFromFilename(filename)
   165→	if shortID == "" {
   166→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Could not extract session ID from filename", "file", filename)
   167→		return
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	// Parse the changed file to get the full session ID
   171→	session, err := ParseSessionFile(filePath)
   172→	if err != nil {
   173→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to parse session file", "file", filePath, "error", err)
   174→		return
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	fullSessionID := session.ID
   178→
   179→	// Find all files for this session to merge them
   180→	// Why: Gemini CLI may have created multiple files for this session
   181→	chatsDir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   182→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
   183→	if err != nil {
   184→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to read chats directory", "dir", chatsDir, "error", err)
   185→		// Fall back to single-file session
   186→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   187→		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   188→		return
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Collect all files that match this session's short ID
   192→	var sessionFiles []string
   193→	for _, entry := range entries {
   194→		if entry.IsDir() {
   195→			continue
   196→		}
   197→		if extractSessionIDFromFilename(entry.Name()) == shortID {
   198→			sessionFiles = append(sessionFiles, filepath.Join(chatsDir, entry.Name()))
   199→		}
   200→	}
   201→
   202→	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Found session files to merge",
   203→		"shortID", shortID,
   204→		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles))
   205→
   206→	// Merge all files for this session
   207→	mergedSession, err := parseAndMergeSessionFiles(shortID, sessionFiles)
   208→	if err != nil {
   209→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to merge session files",
   210→			"sessionId", fullSessionID,
   211→			"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   212→			"error", err)
   213→		// Fall back to single-file session
   214→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   215→		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   216→		return
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	slog.Info("processSessionChange: Successfully processed session change",
   220→		"sessionId", mergedSession.ID,
   221→		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   222→		"messageCount", len(mergedSession.Messages),
   223→		"startTime", mergedSession.StartTime,
   224→		"lastUpdated", mergedSession.LastUpdated)
   225→
   226→	agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(mergedSession, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   227→	triggerCallback(agentSession)
   228→}
   229→
   230→// triggerCallback is a helper to call the watcher callback with proper locking
   231→func triggerCallback(agentSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   232→	watcherMutex.RLock()
   233→	cb := watcherCallback
   234→	watcherMutex.RUnlock()
   235→
   236→	if cb != nil && agentSession != nil {
   237→		cb(agentSession)
   238→	}
   239→}
   240→
   241→func waitForDirectory(ctx context.Context, dir string, label string) error {
   242→	backoff := initialBackoff
   243→	for {
   244→		info, err := os.Stat(dir)
   245→		if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   246→			slog.Info("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
   247→			return nil
   248→		}
   249→		if err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   250→			slog.Warn("Gemini watcher: failed to stat directory", "label", label, "path", dir, "error", err)
   251→		} else {
   252→			slog.Info("Gemini watcher: waiting for directory", "label", label, "path", dir, "retryIn", backoff)
   253→		}
   254→
   255→		select {
   256→		case <-ctx.Done():
   257→			return ctx.Err()
   258→		case <-time.After(backoff):
   259→			if backoff < maxBackoff {
   260→				backoff *= 2
   261→				if backoff > maxBackoff {
   262→					backoff = maxBackoff
   263→				}
   264→			}
   265→		}
   266→	}
   267→}
   268→
   269→func startArtifactWatcher(filePath string, label string) error {
   270→	dir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   271→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   272→	if err != nil {
   273→		return err
   274→	}
   275→
   276→	if err := watcher.Add(dir); err != nil {
   277→		_ = watcher.Close()
   278→		return err
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   282→	go func() {
   283→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   284→		defer func() {
   285→			_ = watcher.Close()
   286→		}()
   287→
   288→		slog.Info("Gemini watcher: monitoring artifact", "artifact", label, "path", filePath)
   289→
   290→		for {
   291→			select {
   292→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   293→				return
   294→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   295→				if !ok {
   296→					return
   297→				}
   298→				if filepath.Clean(event.Name) != filepath.Clean(filePath) {
   299→					continue
   300→				}
   301→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   302→					slog.Info("Gemini artifact updated", "artifact", label, "path", event.Name, "op", event.Op.String())
   303→				}
   304→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   305→				if !ok {
   306→					return
   307→				}
   308→				slog.Warn("Gemini artifact watcher error", "artifact", label, "error", err)
   309→			}
   310→		}
   311→	}()
   312→
   313→	return nil
   314→}
   315→
```

```text
    79→// WatchForProjectDir watches for a project directory that matches the current working directory

    93→
    94→	slog.Info("WatchForProjectDir: Project directory found", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
    95→	return startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir)
    96→}
    97→
    98→// startProjectWatcher starts watching a specific project directory
    99→func startProjectWatcher(claudeProjectDir string) error {
   100→	slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Creating file watcher", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   101→
   102→	// Create a new watcher
   103→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// Increment wait group before starting goroutine
   109→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   110→
   111→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   112→	go func() {
   113→		// Decrement wait group when done
   114→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   115→
   116→		// Log when goroutine starts
   117→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine started")
   118→
   119→		// Defer cleanup
   120→		defer func() {
   121→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Goroutine exiting, closing watcher")
   122→			_ = watcher.Close() // Best effort cleanup; errors here are not recoverable
   123→		}()
   124→
   125→		// Check if project directory already exists
   126→		if _, err := os.Stat(claudeProjectDir); err == nil {
   127→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Project directory exists", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   128→			// Claude Code triggers file system events when it starts writing, so we'll catch the session on the first write event.
   129→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Waiting for JSONL file system events")
   130→		} else {
   131→			// Project directory doesn't exist yet, watch parent directory (or other error)
   132→			parentDir := filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)
   133→			projectName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   134→			slog.Warn("startProjectWatcher: Cannot access project directory, watching parent",
   135→				"error", err,
   136→				"parentDir", parentDir,
   137→				"projectName", projectName)
   138→
   139→			// Add parent directory to watcher
   140→			if err := watcher.Add(parentDir); err != nil {
   141→				log.UserError("Error watching parent directory: %v", err)
   142→				slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to watch parent directory", "error", err)
   143→				return
   144→			}
   145→
   146→			// Wait for project directory to be created
   147→			waitingForCreation := true
   148→			slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now watching for events in parent directory")
   149→			for waitingForCreation {
   150→				select {
   151→				case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   152→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled while waiting for directory creation")
   153→					return
   154→				case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   155→					if !ok {
   156→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher events channel closed")
   157→						return
   158→					}
   159→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Parent directory event",
   160→						"operation", event.Op.String(),
   161→						"path", event.Name,
   162→						"lookingFor", projectName)
   163→					eventBaseName := filepath.Base(event.Name)
   164→					slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Event basename comparison",
   165→						"basename", eventBaseName,
   166→						"comparingWith", projectName,
   167→						"equalFold", strings.EqualFold(eventBaseName, projectName))
   168→					if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), projectName) {
   169→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Detected project directory creation",
   170→							"path", event.Name)
   171→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Initial scan disabled; waiting for JSONL events")
   172→						// Use the actual created directory name from the event
   173→						claudeProjectDir = filepath.Join(parentDir, filepath.Base(event.Name))
   174→						// Remove parent directory and continue to watch project directory
   175→						_ = watcher.Remove(parentDir) // OK if this fails; we're moving to project dir
   176→						waitingForCreation = false
   177→					}
   178→				case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   179→					if !ok {
   180→						slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Watcher errors channel closed")
   181→						return
   182→					}
   183→					log.UserWarn("Watcher error while waiting for project directory: %v", err)
   184→					slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Watcher error", "error", err)
   185→				}
   186→			}
   187→		}
   188→
   189→		// Add the project directory to the watcher
   190→		// Re-stat to get the actual directory name with correct case
   191→		actualProjectDir := claudeProjectDir
   192→		if entries, err := os.ReadDir(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir)); err == nil {
   193→			baseName := filepath.Base(claudeProjectDir)
   194→			for _, entry := range entries {
   195→				if strings.EqualFold(entry.Name(), baseName) {
   196→					actualProjectDir = filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(claudeProjectDir), entry.Name())
   197→					break
   198→				}
   199→			}
   200→		}
   201→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Now monitoring project directory for changes",
   202→			"directory", actualProjectDir)
   203→		if err := watcher.Add(actualProjectDir); err != nil {
   204→			log.UserWarn("Error watching project directory: %v", err)
   205→			slog.Error("startProjectWatcher: Failed to monitor directory", "error", err)
   206→			return
   207→		}
   208→		slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Successfully monitoring project directory for future changes")
   209→
   210→		// Watch for file events
   211→		for {
   212→			select {
   213→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   214→				slog.Info("startProjectWatcher: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   215→				return
   216→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   217→				if !ok {
   218→					return
   219→				}
   220→
   221→				// Only process JSONL files
   222→				if !strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".jsonl") {
   223→					continue
   224→				}
   225→
   226→				if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && !event.Has(fsnotify.Write) {
   227→					continue
   228→				}
   229→
   230→				// Handle different event types
   231→				switch {
   232→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Create):
   233→					slog.Info("New JSONL file created", "file", event.Name)
   234→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   235→				case event.Has(fsnotify.Write):
   236→					slog.Info("JSONL file modified", "file", event.Name)
   237→					slog.Info("Triggering scan due to file modification")
   238→					scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir, event.Name)
   239→				}
   240→
   241→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   242→				if !ok {
   243→					return
   244→				}
   245→				log.UserError("Watcher error: %v", err)
   246→			}
   247→		}
   248→	}()
   249→
   250→	return nil
   251→}
   252→
   253→// scanJSONLFiles scans JSONL files and optionally filters processing to a specific changed file
   254→func scanJSONLFiles(claudeProjectDir string, changedFile ...string) {
   255→	// Ensure logs are flushed even if we panic
   256→	defer func() {
   257→		if r := recover(); r != nil {
   258→			log.UserWarn("PANIC in ScanJSONLFiles: %v", r)
   259→			slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: PANIC recovered", "panic", r)
   260→		}
   261→	}()
   262→
   263→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === START SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   264→	var targetFile string
   265→	if len(changedFile) > 0 && changedFile[0] != "" {
   266→		targetFile = changedFile[0]
   267→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files with changed file",
   268→			"directory", claudeProjectDir,
   269→			"changedFile", targetFile)
   270→	} else {
   271→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Scanning JSONL files", "directory", claudeProjectDir)
   272→	}
   273→	// Parse files and create history files:
   274→	parser := NewJSONLParser()
   275→	var targetSessionUuid string
   276→
   277→	// Set the target file for conditional debug output
   278→	if targetFile != "" {
   279→		SetDebugTargetFile(targetFile)
   280→		defer ClearDebugTargetFile()
   281→
   282→		sessionID, err := extractSessionIDFromFile(targetFile)
   283→		if err != nil {
   284→			slog.Warn("ScanJSONLFiles: Failed to extract session ID from file",
   285→				"file", targetFile,
   286→				"error", err)
   287→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan due to file read error", "file", targetFile)
   288→			return
   289→		}
   290→		if sessionID == "" {
   291→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Skipping scan; session ID not present yet", "file", targetFile)
   292→			return
   293→		}
   294→		targetSessionUuid = sessionID
   295→		slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Extracted session ID from changed file",
   296→			"sessionUuid", targetSessionUuid,
   297→			"file", targetFile)
   298→	}
   299→
   300→	var parseErr error
   301→	if targetSessionUuid != "" {
   302→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessionsForSession(claudeProjectDir, true, targetSessionUuid)
   303→	} else {
   304→		parseErr = parser.ParseProjectSessions(claudeProjectDir, true)
   305→	}
   306→	if parseErr != nil {
   307→		log.UserWarn("Error parsing project sessions: %v", parseErr)
   308→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Error parsing project sessions", "error", parseErr)
   309→		return
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Process JSONL sessions (isAutosave = true, silent = false for autosave)
   313→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions",
   314→		"sessionCount", len(parser.Sessions),
   315→		"targetSessionUuid", targetSessionUuid)
   316→
   317→	// Get the session callback
   318→	callback := getWatcherCallback()
   319→	if callback == nil {
   320→		slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: No callback provided - this should not happen")
   321→		return
   322→	}
   323→
   324→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing sessions with callback")
   325→	// Process sessions and call the callback for each
   326→	for _, session := range parser.Sessions {
   327→		// Skip empty sessions
   328→		if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   329→			continue
   330→		}
   331→
   332→		// Skip if we're targeting a specific session and this isn't it
   333→		if targetSessionUuid != "" && session.SessionUuid != targetSessionUuid {
   334→			continue
   335→		}
   336→
   337→		// Convert to AgentChatSession (workspaceRoot extracted from records' cwd field)
   338→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, "", getWatcherDebugRaw())
   339→		if agentSession != nil {
   340→			slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Calling callback for session", "sessionId", agentSession.SessionID)
   341→			// Call the callback in a goroutine to avoid blocking
   342→			go func(s *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   343→				defer func() {
   344→					if r := recover(); r != nil {
   345→						slog.Error("ScanJSONLFiles: Callback panicked", "panic", r)
   346→					}
   347→				}()
   348→				callback(s)
   349→			}(agentSession)
   350→		}
   351→	}
   352→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: Processing complete")
   353→	slog.Info("ScanJSONLFiles: === END SCAN ===", "timestamp", time.Now().Format(time.RFC3339))
   354→}
   355→
   356→// WatchForClaudeSetup watches for the creation of Claude directories
   357→func WatchForClaudeSetup() error {
   358→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Starting Claude directory setup watcher")
   359→	homeDir, err := os.UserHomeDir()
   360→	if err != nil {
   361→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
   362→	}
   363→
   364→	claudeDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".claude")
   365→	projectsDir := filepath.Join(claudeDir, "projects")
   366→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Directories",
   367→		"homeDir", homeDir,
   368→		"claudeDir", claudeDir,
   369→		"projectsDir", projectsDir)
   370→
   371→	// Determine what to watch
   372→	var watchDir string
   373→	var watchingFor string
   374→
   375→	if _, err := os.Stat(claudeDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   376→		// .claude doesn't exist, watch home directory
   377→		watchDir = homeDir
   378→		watchingFor = ".claude"
   379→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory does not exist")
   380→		slog.Info("Claude directory not found, watching for creation of ~/.claude\n")
   381→	} else {
   382→		// .claude exists but projects doesn't, watch .claude
   383→		watchDir = claudeDir
   384→		watchingFor = "projects"
   385→		slog.Warn("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude directory exists, checking for projects")
   386→		slog.Info("Claude directory found, watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   387→	}
   388→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Will watch directory",
   389→		"watchDir", watchDir,
   390→		"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   391→
   392→	// Create watcher
   393→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   394→	if err != nil {
   395→		log.UserWarn("Failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   396→		slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to create file watcher", "error", err)
   397→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create file watcher: %v", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	// Add directory to watch
   401→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Adding directory to watcher", "directory", watchDir)
   402→	if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   403→		_ = watcher.Close() // Clean up on error; close errors less important than Add error
   404→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to watch directory %s: %v", watchDir, err)
   405→	}
   406→	slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started watching", "directory", watchDir)
   407→
   408→	// Start watching in a goroutine
   409→	go func() {
   410→		defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }() // Cleanup on exit; errors not recoverable
   411→
   412→		for {
   413→			select {
   414→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   415→				if !ok {
   416→					return
   417→				}
   418→
   419→				slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Received event",
   420→					"operation", event.Op.String(),
   421→					"path", event.Name)
   422→				// Check if this is the directory we're waiting for (case-insensitive)
   423→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && strings.EqualFold(filepath.Base(event.Name), watchingFor) {
   424→					slog.Info("Detected creation", "path", event.Name)
   425→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Target directory created", "path", event.Name)
   426→
   427→					// Wait a second to let things settle
   428→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Waiting 1 second for things to settle")
   429→					time.Sleep(1 * time.Second)
   430→
   431→					// Check what we should do next
   432→					if watchingFor == ".claude" {
   433→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: .claude was created, checking if projects exists")
   434→						// We were watching for .claude, now check if projects exists
   435→						if _, err := os.Stat(projectsDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
   436→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory does not exist, switching watcher")
   437→							// Need to watch for projects directory
   438→							_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; switching watch targets
   439→							watchDir = claudeDir
   440→							watchingFor = "projects"
   441→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Switching to watch",
   442→								"watchDir", watchDir,
   443→								"watchingFor", watchingFor)
   444→							if err := watcher.Add(watchDir); err != nil {
   445→								log.UserWarn("Error switching to watch .claude directory: %v", err)
   446→								slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to switch watcher", "error", err)
   447→								return
   448→							}
   449→							slog.Info("Now watching for creation of ~/.claude/projects\n")
   450→							continue
   451→						} else {
   452→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: projects directory already exists!")
   453→						}
   454→					}
   455→
   456→					// If we get here, projects directory should exist
   457→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Projects directory should now exist, cleaning up watcher")
   458→					// Remove the current watcher
   459→					_ = watcher.Remove(watchDir) // OK if fails; we're transitioning to project watcher
   460→
   461→					// Start the normal project watcher
   462→					slog.Info("Claude projects directory now exists, starting normal file watcher\n")
   463→					// Try to get the project directory again
   464→					slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Getting project directory name")
   465→					if projectDir, err := GetClaudeCodeProjectDir(""); err == nil {
   466→						slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Project directory will be", "directory", projectDir)
   467→						if err := startProjectWatcher(projectDir); err != nil {
   468→							log.UserWarn("Error starting project watcher: %v", err)
   469→							slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to start project watcher", "error", err)
   470→						} else {
   471→							slog.Info("WatchForClaudeSetup: Successfully started project watcher")
   472→						}
   473→					} else {
   474→						log.UserWarn("Error getting project directory after Claude setup: %v", err)
   475→						slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Failed to get project directory", "error", err)
   476→					}
   477→					return
   478→				}
   479→
   480→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   481→				if !ok {
   482→					return
   483→				}
   484→				log.UserWarn("Watcher error: %v", err)
   485→				slog.Error("WatchForClaudeSetup: Watcher error", "error", err)
   486→			}
   487→		}
   488→	}()
   489→
   490→	return nil
   491→}
   492→
   493→// convertToAgentChatSession converts a Session to an AgentChatSession
   494→// Returns nil if the session is warmup-only (no real messages)
   495→func convertToAgentChatSession(session Session, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
   496→	// Skip empty sessions (no records)
   497→	if len(session.Records) == 0 {
   498→		return nil
   499→	}
   500→
   501→	// Write debug files first (even for warmup-only sessions)
   502→	_ = make(map[int]int) // recordToFileNumber no longer needed
   503→	if debugRaw {
   504→		// Write debug files
   505→		writeDebugRawFiles(session)
   506→	}
   507→
   508→	// Filter warmup messages
   509→	filteredRecords := filterWarmupMessages(session.Records)
   510→
   511→	// Skip if no real messages remain after filtering warmup
   512→	if len(filteredRecords) == 0 {
   513→		slog.Debug("Skipping warmup-only session", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid)
   514→		return nil
   515→	}
   516→
   517→	// Create session with filtered records
   518→	filteredSession := Session{
   519→		SessionUuid: session.SessionUuid,
   520→		Records:     filteredRecords,
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	// Get timestamp from root record
   524→	rootRecord := filteredSession.Records[0]
   525→	timestamp, ok := rootRecord.Data["timestamp"].(string)
   526→	if !ok {
   527→		slog.Warn("convertToAgentChatSession: No timestamp found in root record")
   528→		return nil
   529→	}
   530→
   531→	// Get the slug
   532→	slug := FileSlugFromRootRecord(filteredSession)
   533→
   534→	// Generate SessionData from filtered session
   535→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(filteredSession, workspaceRoot)
   536→	if err != nil {
   537→		slog.Error("Failed to generate SessionData", "sessionId", session.SessionUuid, "error", err)
   538→		return nil
   539→	}
   540→
   541→	// Convert session records to JSONL format for raw data
   542→	// Note: Uses unfiltered session.Records (not filteredSession.Records) to preserve
   543→	// all records including warmup messages in the raw data for complete audit trail
   544→	var rawDataBuilder strings.Builder
   545→	for _, record := range session.Records {
   546→		jsonBytes, _ := json.Marshal(record.Data)
   547→		rawDataBuilder.Write(jsonBytes)
   548→		rawDataBuilder.WriteString("\n")
   549→	}
   550→
   551→	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
   552→		SessionID:   session.SessionUuid,
   553→		CreatedAt:   timestamp, // ISO 8601 timestamp
   554→		Slug:        slug,
   555→		SessionData: sessionData,
   556→		RawData:     rawDataBuilder.String(),
   557→	}
   558→}
   559→
```

**Claude Code provider** (`WatchForClaudeSetup` at line 357-491 and `startProjectWatcher` at line 99-251): Uses fsnotify to watch the parent directory for creation events.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 22:48:35Z)**_

I can see the problem clearly. Two issues:

**Issue 1 — Log level:** The `waitForDirectory` function at `watcher.go:252` logs at `slog.Info` level. Should be `slog.Debug`.

**Issue 2 — Polling vs. watching:** The Gemini `waitForDirectory` (lines 241-267) uses an active polling loop with exponential backoff (1s → 2s → 4s → ... → 30s cap), forever calling `os.Stat()` and logging. Meanwhile, the Claude Code provider uses fsnotify to watch parent directories reactively — it sits idle until an actual filesystem event fires (see `WatchForClaudeSetup` at lines 357-491 and `startProjectWatcher` at lines 126-186). Zero CPU, zero log noise.

The fix should bring the Gemini provider in line with Claude Code's approach: instead of polling in a loop, use fsnotify to watch whatever parent directory *does* exist, and react to `Create` events as each subdirectory in the chain (`~/.gemini/tmp` → `<hash>` → `chats`) appears.

```text
## Gemini CLI Directory Structure Analysis

Based on my exploration of the SpecStory CLI codebase, here's what I found:

### Directory Structure

The Gemini CLI uses a hierarchical directory structure rooted in the user's home directory:

```
~/.gemini/                          # Gemini root (always exists)
├── tmp/                            # Gemini tmp directory (parent for all projects)
│   ├── <hash-1>/                   # Project-specific dir (SHA256 of canonical project path)
│   │   ├── chats/                  # Contains session JSON files
│   │   │   ├── session-*.json      # Individual session files
│   │   │   └── (can have multiple files per session)
│   │   ├── logs.json
│   │   └── shell_history
│   ├── <hash-2>/
│   │   └── chats/
│   ├── ... (19 projects on your system)
├── history/
├── projects.json
├── settings.json
└── ... (other Gemini files)
```

### Key Functions

**GetGeminiProjectDir()** (path_utils.go:78-108):
- Computes a SHA256 hash of the project path (using canonical path for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
- Returns: `~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/`
- Does NOT verify directories exist

**ResolveGeminiProjectDir()** (path_utils.go:110-168):
- Calls GetGeminiProjectDir()
- Then validates that both `~/.gemini/tmp` and the hash-specific directory exist
- Returns specific GeminiPathError if missing with helpful suggestions
- Calls ListGeminiProjectHashes() to show what hashes are available

**ListGeminiProjectHashes()** (path_utils.go:170-191):
- Reads `~/.gemini/tmp/` directory
- Returns sorted list of directory names (the hashes)

### How WatchGeminiProject Works

The function is called from two places in provider.go:
1. **ExecAgentAndWatch()** (line 165) - Watches while executing Gemini
2. **WatchAgent()** (line 189) - Watches without executing

**WatchGeminiProject()** sequence (watcher.go:71-116):
1. Gets the project dir using GetGeminiProjectDir()
2. Extracts tmpDir = `~/.gemini/tmp`
3. Extracts chatsDir = `~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats`
4. **Calls waitForDirectory() three times with polling** to wait for:
   - tmpDir (1-30 second backoff)
   - projectDir (1-30 second backoff)
   - chatsDir (1-30 second backoff)
5. Once all exist, starts fsnotify watchers on:
   - chatsDir (for *.json files)
   - logs.json (in projectDir)
   - shell_history (in projectDir)

### The Polling Issue

**waitForDirectory()** (watcher.go:241-267):
- Uses exponential backoff: 1s → 2s → 4s → 8s → 16s → 30s (capped)
- Polls with `os.Stat()` every N seconds until directory exists
- This is **synchronous polling** before fsnotify can start
- Reason: Directories may not exist yet when Gemini CLI is starting

### Optimization Opportunity

The key insight: **`~/.gemini/tmp` always exists** (it's the parent directory). You could:

1. **Watch the parent (~/.gemini/tmp)** with fsnotify instead of polling
2. **Detect when the hash directory appears** via fsnotify CREATE event
3. **Detect when chats/ subdirectory appears** via fsnotify CREATE event
4. **Then start the targeted fsnotify watchers** on chats/ and artifacts

This would eliminate the 1-30 second polling delay and respond instantly when directories are created.

### File References

The relevant files are:
- `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/path_utils.go` - Directory resolution logic
- `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/watcher.go` - Polling and fsnotify implementation
- `/Users/sean/Source/SpecStory/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/providers/geminicli/provider.go` - Call sites (lines 165, 189)
```

```text
     1→package geminicli
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"crypto/sha256"
     5→	"encoding/hex"
     6→	"errors"
     7→	"fmt"
     8→	"log/slog"
     9→	"os"
    10→	"path/filepath"
    11→	"sort"
    12→	"strings"
    13→
    14→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"
    15→)
    16→
    17→var (
    18→	osGetwd = os.Getwd
    19→)
    20→
    21→// GeminiPathError describes actionable filesystem failures when locating Gemini data.
    22→type GeminiPathError struct {
    23→	Kind        string   // tmp_missing, project_missing
    24→	Path        string   // offending path
    25→	ProjectHash string   // computed hash when relevant
    26→	KnownHashes []string // hashes discovered on disk (optional)
    27→	Message     string   // user-facing explanation
    28→}
    29→
    30→func (e *GeminiPathError) Error() string {
    31→	if e == nil {
    32→		return "<nil>"
    33→	}
    34→	return e.Message
    35→}
    36→
    37→// HashProjectPath deterministically hashes an absolute project path using SHA256.
    38→// On case-insensitive filesystems (macOS), it resolves the path to its canonical
    39→// form with the correct case to ensure consistent hashing.
    40→func HashProjectPath(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    41→	if projectPath == "" {
    42→		return "", fmt.Errorf("project path is empty")
    43→	}
    44→
    45→	absPath, err := filepath.Abs(projectPath)
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve absolute path for %q: %w", projectPath, err)
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	// Resolve to canonical path with correct case (important for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
    51→	canonicalPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(absPath)
    52→	if err != nil {
    53→		// If getting canonical path fails, use absPath as fallback
    54→		slog.Warn("HashProjectPath: Failed to get canonical path, using absolute path",
    55→			"absPath", absPath,
    56→			"error", err)
    57→		canonicalPath = absPath
    58→	} else if canonicalPath != absPath {
    59→		// Log when the path was canonicalized (case was corrected)
    60→		slog.Debug("HashProjectPath: Resolved path to canonical form",
    61→			"original", absPath,
    62→			"canonical", canonicalPath)
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(canonicalPath))
    66→	return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil
    67→}
    68→
    69→// GetGeminiTmpDir returns the path to the Gemini tmp directory
    70→func GetGeminiTmpDir() (string, error) {
    71→	homeDir, err := osUserHomeDir()
    72→	if err != nil {
    73→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get user home directory: %v", err)
    74→	}
    75→	return filepath.Join(homeDir, ".gemini", "tmp"), nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→// GetGeminiProjectDir returns the Gemini project directory for the given path.
    79→// It calculates the SHA256 hash of the project path.
    80→func GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    81→	if projectPath == "" {
    82→		var err error
    83→		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
    84→		if err != nil {
    85→			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
    86→		}
    87→	}
    88→
    89→	projectHash, err := HashProjectPath(projectPath)
    90→	if err != nil {
    91→		return "", err
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
    95→	if err != nil {
    96→		return "", err
    97→	}
    98→
    99→	resolvedDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, projectHash)
   100→
   101→	slog.Debug("GetGeminiProjectDir: Computed Gemini project directory",
   102→		"projectPath", projectPath,
   103→		"projectHash", projectHash,
   104→		"tmpDir", tmpDir,
   105→		"resolvedDir", resolvedDir)
   106→
   107→	return resolvedDir, nil
   108→}
   109→
   110→// ResolveGeminiProjectDir ensures both tmp and hash directories exist on disk.
   111→func ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
   112→	projectDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
   113→	if err != nil {
   114→		return "", err
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	tmpDir := filepath.Dir(projectDir)
   118→
   119→	slog.Debug("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Checking for Gemini directories",
   120→		"tmpDir", tmpDir,
   121→		"projectDir", projectDir)
   122→
   123→	if _, err := osStat(tmpDir); err != nil {
   124→		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   125→			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini tmp directory not found", "tmpDir", tmpDir)
   126→			return "", &GeminiPathError{
   127→				Kind:    "tmp_missing",
   128→				Path:    tmpDir,
   129→				Message: fmt.Sprintf("Gemini tmp directory %q not found. Run the Gemini CLI at least once or verify ~/.gemini exists.", tmpDir),
   130→			}
   131→		}
   132→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   133→	}
   134→
   135→	if _, err := osStat(projectDir); err != nil {
   136→		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   137→			hashes, listErr := ListGeminiProjectHashes()
   138→			if listErr != nil {
   139→				hashes = nil
   140→			}
   141→
   142→			var known string
   143→			if len(hashes) > 0 {
   144→				known = strings.Join(hashes, ", ")
   145→			} else {
   146→				known = "(none discovered)"
   147→			}
   148→
   149→			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini project directory not found",
   150→				"projectDir", projectDir,
   151→				"knownHashes", known)
   152→
   153→			return "", &GeminiPathError{
   154→				Kind:        "project_missing",
   155→				Path:        projectDir,
   156→				ProjectHash: filepath.Base(projectDir),
   157→				KnownHashes: hashes,
   158→				Message: fmt.Sprintf("No Gemini data found for this project (expected %q). Known project hashes: %s. Start a Gemini CLI session in your repo to create it.",
   159→					projectDir, known),
   160→			}
   161→		}
   162→		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini project directory %q: %w", projectDir, err)
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Successfully resolved Gemini project directory", "projectDir", projectDir)
   166→
   167→	return projectDir, nil
   168→}
   169→
   170→// ListGeminiProjectHashes returns the list of project hash directories currently on disk.
   171→func ListGeminiProjectHashes() ([]string, error) {
   172→	tmpDir, err := GetGeminiTmpDir()
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return nil, err
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
   178→	if err != nil {
   179→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	var hashes []string
   183→	for _, entry := range entries {
   184→		if entry.IsDir() {
   185→			hashes = append(hashes, entry.Name())
   186→		}
   187→	}
   188→
   189→	sort.Strings(hashes)
   190→	return hashes, nil
   191→}
   192→
```

```text
     3→import (

    10→	"sync"

    13→	"github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify"
    14→	"github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/specstory-cli/pkg/spi"

    16→
    17→var (
    18→	watcherCtx           context.Context
    19→	watcherCancel        context.CancelFunc
    20→	watcherWg            sync.WaitGroup
    21→	watcherCallback      func(*spi.AgentChatSession)
    22→	watcherMutex         sync.RWMutex
    23→	watcherDebugRaw      bool
    24→	watcherWorkspaceRoot string
    25→)
    26→
    27→const (
    28→	initialBackoff = 1 * time.Second
    29→	maxBackoff     = 30 * time.Second
    30→)
    31→
    32→func init() {
    33→	watcherCtx, watcherCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    34→}
    35→
    36→func SetWatcherCallback(callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) {
    37→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    38→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    39→	watcherCallback = callback
    40→}
    41→
    42→func SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw bool) {
    43→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    44→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    45→	watcherDebugRaw = debugRaw
    46→}
    47→
    48→func SetWatcherWorkspaceRoot(workspaceRoot string) {
    49→	watcherMutex.Lock()
    50→	defer watcherMutex.Unlock()
    51→	watcherWorkspaceRoot = workspaceRoot
    52→}
    53→
    54→func getWatcherWorkspaceRoot() string {
    55→	watcherMutex.RLock()
    56→	defer watcherMutex.RUnlock()
    57→	return watcherWorkspaceRoot
    58→}
    59→
    60→func getWatcherDebugRaw() bool {
    61→	watcherMutex.RLock()
    62→	defer watcherMutex.RUnlock()
    63→	return watcherDebugRaw
    64→}
    65→
    66→func StopWatcher() {
    67→	watcherCancel()
    68→	watcherWg.Wait()
    69→}
    70→
    71→func WatchGeminiProject(projectPath string, callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    72→	SetWatcherCallback(callback)
    73→	SetWatcherWorkspaceRoot(projectPath)
    74→
    75→	projectDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
    76→	if err != nil {
    77→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get gemini project dir: %w", err)
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	tmpDir := filepath.Dir(projectDir)
    81→	chatsDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, "chats")
    82→
    83→	watcherWg.Add(1)
    84→	go func() {
    85→		defer watcherWg.Done()
    86→
    87→		if err := waitForDirectory(watcherCtx, tmpDir, "Gemini tmp directory"); err != nil {
    88→			slog.Warn("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for tmp directory", "error", err)
    89→			return
    90→		}
    91→
    92→		if err := waitForDirectory(watcherCtx, projectDir, "Gemini project directory"); err != nil {
    93→			slog.Warn("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for project directory", "error", err)
    94→			return
    95→		}
    96→
    97→		if err := waitForDirectory(watcherCtx, chatsDir, "Gemini chats directory"); err != nil {
    98→			slog.Warn("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for chats directory", "error", err)
    99→			return
   100→		}
   101→
   102→		if err := startChatsWatcher(chatsDir); err != nil {
   103→			slog.Error("Failed to start chats watcher", "error", err)
   104→		}
   105→
   106→		if err := startArtifactWatcher(filepath.Join(projectDir, "logs.json"), "logs.json"); err != nil {
   107→			slog.Warn("Failed to watch logs.json", "error", err)
   108→		}
   109→
   110→		if err := startArtifactWatcher(filepath.Join(projectDir, "shell_history"), "shell_history"); err != nil {
   111→			slog.Warn("Failed to watch shell_history", "error", err)
   112→		}
   113→	}()
   114→
   115→	return nil
   116→}
   117→
   118→func startChatsWatcher(chatsDir string) error {
   119→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   120→	if err != nil {
   121→		return err
   122→	}
   123→
   124→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   125→	go func() {
   126→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   127→		defer func() {
   128→			_ = watcher.Close()
   129→		}()
   130→
   131→		if err := watcher.Add(chatsDir); err != nil {
   132→			slog.Error("Failed to add chats dir to watcher", "error", err)
   133→			return
   134→		}
   135→
   136→		for {
   137→			select {
   138→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   139→				return
   140→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   141→				if !ok {
   142→					return
   143→				}
   144→				if strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".json") && (event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create)) {
   145→					processSessionChange(event.Name)
   146→				}
   147→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   148→				if !ok {
   149→					return
   150→				}
   151→				slog.Error("Watcher error", "error", err)
   152→			}
   153→		}
   154→	}()
   155→
   156→	return nil
   157→}
   158→
   159→func processSessionChange(filePath string) {
   160→	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Detected session file change", "file", filePath)
   161→
   162→	// Extract short session ID from filename to find related files
   163→	filename := filepath.Base(filePath)
   164→	shortID := extractSessionIDFromFilename(filename)
   165→	if shortID == "" {
   166→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Could not extract session ID from filename", "file", filename)
   167→		return
   168→	}
   169→
   170→	// Parse the changed file to get the full session ID
   171→	session, err := ParseSessionFile(filePath)
   172→	if err != nil {
   173→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to parse session file", "file", filePath, "error", err)
   174→		return
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	fullSessionID := session.ID
   178→
   179→	// Find all files for this session to merge them
   180→	// Why: Gemini CLI may have created multiple files for this session
   181→	chatsDir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   182→	entries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
   183→	if err != nil {
   184→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to read chats directory", "dir", chatsDir, "error", err)
   185→		// Fall back to single-file session
   186→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   187→		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   188→		return
   189→	}
   190→
   191→	// Collect all files that match this session's short ID
   192→	var sessionFiles []string
   193→	for _, entry := range entries {
   194→		if entry.IsDir() {
   195→			continue
   196→		}
   197→		if extractSessionIDFromFilename(entry.Name()) == shortID {
   198→			sessionFiles = append(sessionFiles, filepath.Join(chatsDir, entry.Name()))
   199→		}
   200→	}
   201→
   202→	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Found session files to merge",
   203→		"shortID", shortID,
   204→		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles))
   205→
   206→	// Merge all files for this session
   207→	mergedSession, err := parseAndMergeSessionFiles(shortID, sessionFiles)
   208→	if err != nil {
   209→		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to merge session files",
   210→			"sessionId", fullSessionID,
   211→			"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   212→			"error", err)
   213→		// Fall back to single-file session
   214→		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   215→		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   216→		return
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	slog.Info("processSessionChange: Successfully processed session change",
   220→		"sessionId", mergedSession.ID,
   221→		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   222→		"messageCount", len(mergedSession.Messages),
   223→		"startTime", mergedSession.StartTime,
   224→		"lastUpdated", mergedSession.LastUpdated)
   225→
   226→	agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(mergedSession, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   227→	triggerCallback(agentSession)
   228→}
   229→
   230→// triggerCallback is a helper to call the watcher callback with proper locking
   231→func triggerCallback(agentSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   232→	watcherMutex.RLock()
   233→	cb := watcherCallback
   234→	watcherMutex.RUnlock()
   235→
   236→	if cb != nil && agentSession != nil {
   237→		cb(agentSession)
   238→	}
   239→}
   240→
   241→func waitForDirectory(ctx context.Context, dir string, label string) error {
   242→	backoff := initialBackoff
   243→	for {
   244→		info, err := os.Stat(dir)
   245→		if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   246→			slog.Info("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
   247→			return nil
   248→		}
   249→		if err != nil && !os.IsNotExist(err) {
   250→			slog.Warn("Gemini watcher: failed to stat directory", "label", label, "path", dir, "error", err)
   251→		} else {
   252→			slog.Info("Gemini watcher: waiting for directory", "label", label, "path", dir, "retryIn", backoff)
   253→		}
   254→
   255→		select {
   256→		case <-ctx.Done():
   257→			return ctx.Err()
   258→		case <-time.After(backoff):
   259→			if backoff < maxBackoff {
   260→				backoff *= 2
   261→				if backoff > maxBackoff {
   262→					backoff = maxBackoff
   263→				}
   264→			}
   265→		}
   266→	}
   267→}
   268→
   269→func startArtifactWatcher(filePath string, label string) error {
   270→	dir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   271→	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   272→	if err != nil {
   273→		return err
   274→	}
   275→
   276→	if err := watcher.Add(dir); err != nil {
   277→		_ = watcher.Close()
   278→		return err
   279→	}
   280→
   281→	watcherWg.Add(1)
   282→	go func() {
   283→		defer watcherWg.Done()
   284→		defer func() {
   285→			_ = watcher.Close()
   286→		}()
   287→
   288→		slog.Info("Gemini watcher: monitoring artifact", "artifact", label, "path", filePath)
   289→
   290→		for {
   291→			select {
   292→			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   293→				return
   294→			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   295→				if !ok {
   296→					return
   297→				}
   298→				if filepath.Clean(event.Name) != filepath.Clean(filePath) {
   299→					continue
   300→				}
   301→				if event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   302→					slog.Info("Gemini artifact updated", "artifact", label, "path", event.Name, "op", event.Op.String())
   303→				}
   304→			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   305→				if !ok {
   306→					return
   307→				}
   308→				slog.Warn("Gemini artifact watcher error", "artifact", label, "error", err)
   309→			}
   310→		}
   311→	}()
   312→
   313→	return nil
   314→}
   315→
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
    30→func (p *Provider) Check(customCommand string) spi.CheckResult {
    31→	cmdName, _ := parseGeminiCommand(customCommand)
    32→	isCustom := customCommand != ""
    33→
    34→	resolvedPath, err := exec.LookPath(cmdName)
    35→	if err != nil {
    36→		errorMessage := buildGeminiCheckErrorMessage("not_found", cmdName, isCustom, "")
    37→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
    38→			"provider":       "gemini",
    39→			"custom_command": isCustom,
    40→			"command_path":   cmdName,
    41→			"error_type":     "not_found",
    42→			"error_message":  err.Error(),
    43→		})
    44→		return spi.CheckResult{
    45→			Success:      false,
    46→			Location:     "",
    47→			ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
    48→		}
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	cmd := exec.Command(cmdName, "--version")
    52→	var stdout bytes.Buffer
    53→	var stderr bytes.Buffer
    54→	cmd.Stdout = &stdout
    55→	cmd.Stderr = &stderr
    56→
    57→	if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
    58→		errorType := classifyGeminiCheckError(err)
    59→		errorMessage := buildGeminiCheckErrorMessage(errorType, resolvedPath, isCustom, strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String()))
    60→		analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallFailed, analytics.Properties{
    61→			"provider":       "gemini",
    62→			"custom_command": isCustom,
    63→			"command_path":   resolvedPath,
    64→			"error_type":     errorType,
    65→			"error_message":  err.Error(),
    66→		})
    67→
    68→		return spi.CheckResult{
    69→			Success:      false,
    70→			Location:     resolvedPath,
    71→			ErrorMessage: errorMessage,
    72→		}
    73→	}
    74→
    75→	version := strings.TrimSpace(stdout.String())
    76→	analytics.TrackEvent(analytics.EventCheckInstallSuccess, analytics.Properties{
    77→		"provider":       "gemini",
    78→		"custom_command": isCustom,
    79→		"command_path":   resolvedPath,
    80→		"version":        version,

    83→	return spi.CheckResult{

    90→func (p *Provider) DetectAgent(projectPath string, helpOutput bool) bool {

   112→func (p *Provider) GetAgentChatSessions(projectPath string, debugRaw bool, progress spi.ProgressCallback) ([]spi.AgentChatSession, error) {

   160→func (p *Provider) ExecAgentAndWatch(projectPath string, customCommand string, resumeSessionID string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   161→	slog.Info("ExecAgentAndWatch: Starting Gemini CLI", "project", projectPath)
   162→
   163→	// Start watching
   164→	SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
   165→	if err := WatchGeminiProject(projectPath, sessionCallback); err != nil {
   166→		slog.Error("Failed to start watcher", "error", err)
   167→	}
   168→	defer StopWatcher()
   169→
   170→	if resumeSessionID != "" {
   171→		slog.Info("Attempting to resume Gemini CLI session", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
   172→	}
   173→
   174→	return ExecuteGemini(customCommand, resumeSessionID)
   175→}
   176→
   177→// WatchAgent watches for Gemini CLI agent activity and calls the callback with AgentChatSession
   178→// Does NOT execute the agent - only watches for existing activity
   179→// Runs until error or context cancellation (blocks indefinitely)
   180→func (p *Provider) WatchAgent(ctx context.Context, projectPath string, debugRaw bool, sessionCallback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
   181→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Starting Gemini CLI activity monitoring",
   182→		"projectPath", projectPath,
   183→		"debugRaw", debugRaw)
   184→
   185→	// Set up debug mode
   186→	SetWatcherDebugRaw(debugRaw)
   187→
   188→	// Start watching for Gemini sessions
   189→	if err := WatchGeminiProject(projectPath, sessionCallback); err != nil {
   190→		slog.Error("WatchAgent: Failed to start Gemini session watcher", "error", err)
   191→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start watcher: %w", err)
   192→	}
   193→
   194→	// Block until context is cancelled
   195→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Watcher started, blocking until context cancelled")
   196→	<-ctx.Done()
   197→
   198→	slog.Info("WatchAgent: Context cancelled, stopping watcher")
   199→	StopWatcher()
   200→
   201→	return ctx.Err()
   202→}
   203→
   204→func classifyGeminiCheckError(err error) string {
   205→	var execErr *exec.Error
   206→	var pathErr *os.PathError
   207→
   208→	switch {
   209→	case errors.As(err, &execErr) && execErr.Err == exec.ErrNotFound:
   210→		return "not_found"
   211→	case errors.As(err, &pathErr):
   212→		if errors.Is(pathErr.Err, os.ErrPermission) {
   213→			return "permission_denied"
   214→		}
   215→	case errors.Is(err, os.ErrPermission):
   216→		return "permission_denied"
   217→	}
   218→	return "version_failed"
   219→}
   220→
   221→func buildGeminiCheckErrorMessage(errorType string, geminiCmd string, isCustom bool, stderr string) string {
   222→	var b strings.Builder
   223→
   224→	switch errorType {
   225→	case "not_found":
   226→		b.WriteString("Gemini CLI could not be found.\n\n")
   227→		if isCustom {
   228→			b.WriteString("• Verify the path you supplied actually points to the `gemini` executable.\n")
   229→			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "• Provided command: %s\n", geminiCmd)
   230→		} else {
   231→			b.WriteString("• Install Gemini CLI from https://ai.google.dev/gemini-cli/get-started\n")
   232→			b.WriteString("• Ensure `gemini` is on your PATH or pass a custom command via `specstory check gemini -c \"path/to/gemini\"`.\n")
   233→		}
   234→	case "permission_denied":
   235→		b.WriteString("Gemini CLI exists but isn't executable.\n\n")
   236→		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "• Fix permissions: `chmod +x %s`\n", geminiCmd)
   237→		b.WriteString("• Some package managers install the binary as root; run SpecStory with a path you can execute.\n")
   238→	default:
   239→		b.WriteString("`gemini --version` failed.\n\n")
   240→		if stderr != "" {
   241→			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "Error output:\n%s\n\n", stderr)
   242→		}
   243→		b.WriteString("• Try running `gemini --version` directly in your terminal.\n")
   244→		b.WriteString("• If you upgraded recently, reinstall the CLI to refresh dependencies.\n")
   245→	}
   246→
   247→	return b.String()
   248→}
   249→
   250→func printGeminiDetectionHelp(err error) {
   251→	var pathErr *GeminiPathError
   252→	if errors.As(err, &pathErr) {
   253→		switch pathErr.Kind {
   254→		case "tmp_missing":
   255→			log.UserWarn("Gemini tmp directory missing (%s).", pathErr.Path)
   256→			log.UserMessage("Run the Gemini CLI once (e.g., `gemini`) so ~/.gemini/tmp is created, then rerun this command.\n")
   257→		case "project_missing":
   258→			log.UserWarn("No Gemini data found for this project (expected hash %s at %s).", pathErr.ProjectHash, pathErr.Path)
   259→			if len(pathErr.KnownHashes) > 0 {
   260→				log.UserMessage("Known Gemini project hashes: %s\n", strings.Join(pathErr.KnownHashes, ", "))
   261→			} else {
   262→				log.UserMessage("No Gemini projects detected yet under ~/.gemini/tmp.\n")
   263→			}
   264→			log.UserMessage("Start a Gemini CLI session from your repo so the provider can pick it up.\n")
   265→		default:
   266→			log.UserWarn("Gemini detection failed: %v", err)
   267→		}
   268→		return
   269→	}
   270→
   271→	log.UserWarn("Gemini detection failed: %v", err)
   272→}
   273→
   274→// convertToAgentChatSession converts a GeminiSession to the provider-agnostic AgentChatSession format.
   275→// Used by both sync mode (GetAgentChatSession/GetAgentChatSessions) and watch mode.
   276→func convertToAgentChatSession(session *GeminiSession, workspaceRoot string, debugRaw bool) *spi.AgentChatSession {
   277→	// Generate structured session data
   278→	sessionData, err := GenerateAgentSession(session, workspaceRoot)
   279→	if err != nil {
   280→		slog.Error("convertToAgentChatSession: failed to generate session data",
   281→			"sessionId", session.ID,
   282→			"error", err)
   283→		return nil
   284→	}
   285→
   286→	// Extract slug from first user message
   287→	var slug string
   288→	for _, msg := range session.Messages {
   289→		if msg.Type == "user" {
   290→			slug = spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(msgContent(msg))
   291→			break
   292→		}
   293→	}
   294→	if slug == "" {
   295→		slug = "gemini-session"
   296→	}
   297→
   298→	// Raw data
   299→	rawDataBytes, _ := json.Marshal(session)
   300→
   301→	// Write provider-specific debug files if requested
   302→	if debugRaw {
   303→		if err := writeDebugRawFiles(session); err != nil {
   304→			slog.Debug("convertToAgentChatSession: failed to write debug files",
   305→				"sessionId", session.ID,
   306→				"error", err)
   307→		}
   308→	}
   309→
   310→	return &spi.AgentChatSession{
   311→		SessionID:   session.ID,
   312→		CreatedAt:   session.StartTime,
   313→		Slug:        slug,
   314→		SessionData: sessionData,
   315→		RawData:     string(rawDataBytes),
   316→	}
   317→}
   318→
   319→// writeDebugRawFiles writes debug JSON files for a Gemini CLI session.
   320→// Each message is written as a numbered JSON file in .specstory/debug/<session-id>/
   321→func writeDebugRawFiles(session *GeminiSession) error {
   322→	debugDir := spi.GetDebugDir(session.ID)
   323→	if err := os.MkdirAll(debugDir, 0o755); err != nil {
   324→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create debug dir: %w", err)
   325→	}
   326→
   327→	for idx, msg := range session.Messages {
   328→		number := idx + 1
   329→		entry := map[string]interface{}{
   330→			"index":      number,
   331→			"type":       msg.Type,
   332→			"timestamp":  msg.Timestamp,
   333→			"model":      msg.Model,
   334→			"content":    msgContent(msg),
   335→			"rawContent": msg.Content,
   336→			"toolCalls":  msg.ToolCalls,
   337→			"thoughts":   msg.Thoughts,
   338→			"tokens":     msg.Tokens,
   339→			"logs":       session.LogsForMessage(msgContent(msg)),
   340→		}
   341→
   342→		data, err := json.MarshalIndent(entry, "", "  ")
   343→		if err != nil {
   344→			slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: failed to marshal", "index", number, "error", err)
   345→			continue
   346→		}
   347→
   348→		filename := filepath.Join(debugDir, fmt.Sprintf("%d.json", number))
   349→		if err := os.WriteFile(filename, data, 0o644); err != nil {
   350→			slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: failed to write", "index", number, "error", err)
   351→			continue
   352→		}
   353→		slog.Debug("writeDebugRawFiles: wrote file", "path", filename, "index", number)
   354→	}
   355→	return nil
   356→}
   357→
   358→// ListAgentChatSessions retrieves lightweight session metadata without full parsing
   359→func (p *Provider) ListAgentChatSessions(projectPath string) ([]spi.SessionMetadata, error) {
   360→	projectDir, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
   361→	if err != nil {
   362→		return nil, err
   363→	}
   364→
   365→	sessions, err := FindSessions(projectDir)
   366→	if err != nil {
   367→		return nil, err
   368→	}
   369→
   370→	// Extract metadata from each session
   371→	result := make([]spi.SessionMetadata, 0, len(sessions))
   372→	for _, session := range sessions {
   373→		metadata := extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session)
   374→		if metadata == nil {
   375→			slog.Debug("Skipping empty session", "sessionID", session.ID)
   376→			continue
   377→		}
   378→		result = append(result, *metadata)
   379→	}
   380→
   381→	return result, nil
   382→}
   383→
   384→// extractGeminiSessionMetadata extracts lightweight metadata from a Gemini session
   385→// Returns nil if the session has no user messages
   386→func extractGeminiSessionMetadata(session *GeminiSession) *spi.SessionMetadata {
   387→	// Find first user message
   388→	var firstUserMessage string
   389→	for _, msg := range session.Messages {
   390→		if msg.Type == "user" {
   391→			firstUserMessage = msgContent(msg)
   392→			if firstUserMessage != "" {
   393→				break
   394→			}
   395→		}
   396→	}
   397→
   398→	// If no user message found, session is empty
   399→	if firstUserMessage == "" {
   400→		return nil
   401→	}
   402→
   403→	// Generate slug from first user message
   404→	slug := spi.GenerateFilenameFromUserMessage(firstUserMessage)
   405→	if slug == "" {
   406→		slug = "gemini-session"
   407→	}
   408→
   409→	// Generate human-readable name from first user message
   410→	name := spi.GenerateReadableName(firstUserMessage)
   411→
   412→	return &spi.SessionMetadata{
   413→		SessionID: session.ID,
   414→		CreatedAt: session.StartTime,
   415→		Slug:      slug,
   416→		Name:      name,
   417→	}
   418→}
   419→
```

```text
    21→// getDefaultGeminiCommand returns the default gemini command.
    22→// It checks for gemini in PATH.
    23→func getDefaultGeminiCommand() string {
    24→	if _, err := execLookPath("gemini"); err == nil {
    25→		slog.Info("Found Gemini CLI in PATH")
    26→		return "gemini"
    27→	}
    28→	slog.Info("Gemini CLI not found in PATH, defaulting to 'gemini'")
    29→	return "gemini"
    30→}
    31→
    32→// parseGeminiCommand parses a custom command string into executable and arguments.
    33→func parseGeminiCommand(customCommand string) (string, []string) {
    34→	if customCommand != "" {
    35→		parts := spi.SplitCommandLine(customCommand)
    36→		if len(parts) > 0 {
    37→			return parts[0], parts[1:]
    38→		}
    39→	}
    40→	return getDefaultGeminiCommand(), nil
    41→}
    42→
    43→func ensureResumeArgs(args []string, resumeSessionID string) []string {
    44→	if resumeSessionID == "" {
    45→		return args
    46→	}
    47→
    48→	for i, arg := range args {
    49→		if arg == "--resume" || arg == "-r" {
    50→			if i+1 < len(args) {
    51→				return args
    52→			}
    53→		}
    54→		if strings.HasPrefix(arg, "--resume=") {
    55→			return args
    56→		}
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	return append(args, "--resume", resumeSessionID)
    60→}
    61→
    62→// ExecuteGemini runs the Gemini CLI with the given arguments
    63→func ExecuteGemini(customCommand string, resumeSessionID string) error {
    64→	// Parse the command and any custom arguments
    65→	geminiCmd, customArgs := parseGeminiCommand(customCommand)
    66→
    67→	customArgs = ensureResumeArgs(customArgs, resumeSessionID)
    68→	if resumeSessionID != "" {
    69→		slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Passing resume argument", "sessionId", resumeSessionID)
    70→	}
    71→
    72→	// Create the command
    73→	cmd := exec.Command(geminiCmd, customArgs...)
    74→
    75→	// Set up stdin/stdout/stderr to match the parent process
    76→	cmd.Stdin = os.Stdin
    77→	cmd.Stdout = os.Stdout
    78→	cmd.Stderr = os.Stderr
    79→
    80→	// Start the command
    81→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Starting Gemini CLI process")
    82→	if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
    83→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to start gemini: %v", err)
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	// Wait for the command to complete
    87→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Waiting for Gemini CLI to exit")
    88→	if err := cmd.Wait(); err != nil {
    89→		if exitErr, ok := err.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
    90→			exitCode := exitErr.ExitCode()
    91→			slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Gemini CLI exited", "exitCode", exitCode)
    92→			os.Exit(exitCode)
    93→		}
    94→		return fmt.Errorf("gemini execution failed: %v", err)
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	slog.Info("ExecuteGemini: Gemini CLI exited normally", "exitCode", 0)
    98→	return nil
    99→}
   100→
```
